pattern: Because the conceptually endless array is just one fixed block repeated, any shortest matching run either sits inside two consecutive copies of that block or is built from whole copies plus one such run, so reducing the target modulo the block sum and sliding a window over the block doubled once is enough.
hints: Nothing about wrapping past the end of the array changes the sum of a run that lives entirely inside two back-to-back copies of it, so line up two copies and slide a window across that.
Split the target into how many full copies of the block it can absorb and what remainder is left over; the remainder is what you actually search for with the window.

When the remainder equals the whole block sum exactly, drop one full copy from your count and search for the whole block itself instead, since a window can't usefully be longer than one block when a positive-only sliding search is used.

edge_inputs.description: single-element block can never reach an unreachable remainder
target equals the whole block sum, needing a full traversal
shortest run is a single matching element
smallest possible block value repeated many times to reach the target
all-equal block with a target requiring several elements
alternating block where every adjacent pair sums the same, hiding the true shortest run
every block element exceeds the target, so no run can ever match it

stress.description: large random block with a random target across the full constraint range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    target = rng.randint(1, 10 ** 9)
    return [nums, target]

stress.description: large uniform block whose target parity makes a match impossible, forcing a full scan to conclude -1
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [2] * n
    target = 2 * rng.randint(1, 10 ** 8) + 1
    return [nums, target]
